name: GitHub
description: Manage repos, issues, pull requests, code search, file operations, and workflows
setupSteps: Click the link below to create a new token on GitHub
Select scopes: repo, read:org, read:user
Click 'Generate token' and copy it

name: Stripe
description: Manage customers, payments, subscriptions, invoices, and financial data
setupSteps: Click the link below to open your Stripe API keys
Copy the 'Secret key' (starts with sk_live_ or sk_test_)
Use a test key (sk_test_) for safe experimentation

name: Google Calendar
description: Create, list, and update calendar events, check availability, manage schedules
setupSteps: Click 'Sign in with Google' below — your browser will open
Sign in and allow access to your calendar
That's it — token refresh is handled automatically

name: Gmail
description: Send, read, search, and draft emails via Gmail
setupSteps: Click 'Sign in with Google' below — your browser will open
Sign in and allow access to your Gmail
That's it — token refresh is handled automatically
agentInstructions:
## Gmail Integration

You have Gmail connected via GROOVE.

### API
`POST http://localhost:31415/api/integrations/gmail/exec`
Body: `{"tool": "<tool>", "params": {...}}`

### Common Tools
- `send_email` — params: {to, subject, body}
- `search_emails` — params: {query}
- `get_email` — params: {id}
- `draft_email` — params: {to, subject, body}

### Rules
- Always confirm recipient and subject before sending
- Use search before replying to find the thread
- Draft emails in a professional tone unless told otherwise

name: PostgreSQL
description: Query databases, inspect schemas, run SQL, and analyze data
setupSteps: Enter your PostgreSQL connection string
Format: postgresql://username:password@host:port/database
agentInstructions:
## PostgreSQL Integration

You have PostgreSQL connected via GROOVE.

### API
`POST http://localhost:31415/api/integrations/postgres/exec`
Body: `{"tool": "<tool>", "params": {...}}`

### Common Tools
- `query` — params: {sql}
- `list_tables` — params: {}
- `describe_table` — params: {table}

### Rules
- Never run DROP, TRUNCATE, or DELETE without explicit confirmation
- Prefer SELECT queries for exploration
- Use LIMIT on large tables

name: Brave Search
description: Web search and local search via Brave Search API
setupSteps: Click the link below to get a Brave Search API key
Sign up for a free plan (2,000 queries/month)
Copy your API key
agentInstructions:
## Brave Search Integration

You have Brave Search connected via GROOVE.

### API
`POST http://localhost:31415/api/integrations/brave-search/exec`
Body: `{"tool": "<tool>", "params": {...}}`

### Common Tools
- `brave_web_search` — params: {query, count}
- `brave_local_search` — params: {query}

### Rules
- Use specific, targeted queries for best results
- Summarize findings rather than dumping raw results

name: Google Drive
description: Search, read, and upload files to Google Drive with auto-conversion to Slides/Docs/Sheets
setupSteps: Click 'Connect with Google' below — your browser will open
Sign in and allow access to your Drive
That's it — credentials are encrypted and stored locally
agentInstructions:
## Google Drive Integration

You have Google Drive connected via GROOVE.

### Search & Read (MCP)
`POST http://localhost:31415/api/integrations/google-drive/exec`
Body: `{"tool": "<tool>", "params": {...}}`
- `search_files` — params: {query}
- `get_file` — params: {fileId}
- `list_files` — params: {folderId}

### Upload (GROOVE API)
`POST http://localhost:31415/api/integrations/google-drive/upload`
Body: `{"filePath": "/absolute/path/to/file.pptx", "name": "My Deck", "convert": true}`

Auto-converts on upload:
- `.pptx` → Google Slides
- `.docx` → Google Docs
- `.xlsx` → Google Sheets
- `.csv` → Google Sheets
- `.html` → Google Docs

Response: `{"id": "...", "name": "...", "webViewLink": "https://..."}`

Requires approval — you'll get an `approvalId` to retry with.

### Rules
- Build files locally first (PptxGenJS, etc.), then upload
- Use `convert: true` (default) for native Google format
- Use `convert: false` to keep original format in Drive

name: Linear
description: Create and manage issues, projects, and sprints in Linear
setupSteps: Click the link below to open Linear API settings
Click 'Create key', give it a label like 'Groove'
Copy the API key

name: Notion
description: Read, create, and update Notion pages and databases
setupSteps: Click the link below to open Notion integrations
Click 'New integration', name it 'Groove'
Copy the 'Internal Integration Secret'
Share the pages/databases you want accessible with the integration

name: Home Assistant
description: Control smart home devices, query states, and manage automations
setupSteps: Enter your Home Assistant URL (usually http://homeassistant.local:8123)
In HA: Profile (bottom left) > Security > Long-Lived Access Tokens
Click 'Create Token', name it 'Groove', copy the token
agentInstructions:
## Home Assistant Integration

You have Home Assistant connected via GROOVE.

### API
`POST http://localhost:31415/api/integrations/home-assistant/exec`
Body: `{"tool": "<tool>", "params": {...}}`

### Common Tools
- `get_states` — params: {}
- `call_service` — params: {domain, service, entity_id}
- `get_entity` — params: {entity_id}

### Rules
- Confirm before toggling devices or running automations
- Check current state before changing it

name: Google Docs
description: Search and read Google Docs documents (read-only via Drive API)
setupSteps: Click 'Connect with Google' below — your browser will open
Sign in and allow access to your Docs
That's it — credentials are encrypted and stored locally
agentInstructions:
## Google Docs Integration

You have Google Docs connected via GROOVE.

### Search & Read
`POST http://localhost:31415/api/integrations/google-docs/exec`
- `search_files` — params: {query}
- `get_file` — params: {fileId}

### Create (via Drive Upload)
To create a Google Doc, build a `.docx` or `.html` file locally, then upload:
`POST http://localhost:31415/api/integrations/google-drive/upload`
Body: `{"filePath": "/path/to/doc.docx", "convert": true}`
This auto-converts to a native Google Doc.

name: Google Sheets
description: Search and read Google Sheets spreadsheets (read-only via Drive API)
setupSteps: Click 'Connect with Google' below — your browser will open
Sign in and allow access to your Sheets
That's it — credentials are encrypted and stored locally
agentInstructions:
## Google Sheets Integration

You have Google Sheets connected via GROOVE.

### Search & Read
`POST http://localhost:31415/api/integrations/google-sheets/exec`
- `search_files` — params: {query}
- `get_file` — params: {fileId}

### Create (via Drive Upload)
To create a Google Sheet, build a `.xlsx` or `.csv` file locally, then upload:
`POST http://localhost:31415/api/integrations/google-drive/upload`
Body: `{"filePath": "/path/to/data.xlsx", "convert": true}`
This auto-converts to a native Google Sheet.

name: Google Slides
description: Search and read Google Slides presentations (read-only via Drive API)
setupSteps: Click 'Connect with Google' below — your browser will open
Sign in and allow access to your Slides
That's it — credentials are encrypted and stored locally
agentInstructions:
## Google Slides Integration

You have Google Slides connected via GROOVE.

### Search & Read
`POST http://localhost:31415/api/integrations/google-slides/exec`
- `search_files` — params: {query}
- `get_file` — params: {fileId}

### Create (via Drive Upload)
To create a Google Slides deck, build a `.pptx` file locally (e.g., with PptxGenJS), then upload:
`POST http://localhost:31415/api/integrations/google-drive/upload`
Body: `{"filePath": "/path/to/deck.pptx", "convert": true}`
This auto-converts to a native Google Slides presentation.

## ElevenLabs Integration

- Keep text under 5000 chars per request
